title: Endoscope cleaner
abstract: Methods and devices for cleaning heads of tubular instruments, such as endoscopes, are disclosed. A cleaning apparatus can include a receiving block for receiving an instrument head. A clamp can be used to secure the tube of the tubular instrument while also establishing a watertight seal. A nozzle assembly underneath the receiving block can include a nozzle that directs a stream of fluid onto the instrument head. The nozzle assembly can be rotatable about an axis of rotation, and optionally slidable along the axis of rotation. Extent of rotation and sliding movement can be limited (e.g., via one or more stops). Through movement of the nozzle assembly, the stream of fluid can be directed into various openings and crevices of the instrument head, thus permitting a quick and easy way to thoroughly clean such instrument heads.
claims: Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. A cleaning apparatus, comprising: a housing having a receiving space for receiving an instrument head of a tubular instrument; a clamp coupled to the housing and movable between an open position and a closed position, wherein the clamp forms a watertight seal around a tube of the tubular instrument when in the closed position; a nozzle assembly positioned within the housing and movable between a first position and a second position, the nozzle assembly comprising a nozzle for directing a stream of fluid towards a working face of the instrument head, wherein the nozzle directs the stream of fluid towards a first portion of the working face of the instrument head when the nozzle assembly is in the first position, and wherein the nozzle directs the stream of fluid towards a second portion of the working face of the instrument head when the nozzle assembly is in the second position. 2. The cleaning apparatus of claim 1, wherein the housing comprises a receiving block containing the receiving space and a collection chamber fluidly coupled to the receiving space to collect effluent from the stream of fluid contacting the working face of the instrument head. 3. The cleaning apparatus of claim 2, wherein the nozzle assembly is positioned within the collection chamber. 4. The cleaning apparatus of claim 1, wherein the receiving space is sized to accept an end of an endoscope. 5. The cleaning apparatus of claim 1, wherein the receiving space comprises a securing feature for securing the instrument head within the receiving space. 6. The cleaning apparatus of claim 1, wherein the nozzle assembly is rotatable about an axis of rotation. 7. The cleaning apparatus of claim 6, wherein the axis of rotation is orthogonal to an axial direction of the instrument head. 8. The cleaning apparatus of claim 6, wherein the nozzle assembly is translatable in a direction parallel the axis of rotation. 9. The cleaning apparatus of claim 8, wherein the nozzle assembly is translatable between a first endpoint and a second endpoint, wherein the distance between the first endpoint and the second endpoint is less than a diameter of the instrument head. 10. The cleaning apparatus of claim 9, wherein the distance between the first endpoint and the second endpoint is correlated to a width of an elevator portion of the instrument head. 11. The cleaning apparatus of claim 1, further comprising a manual control interface coupled to the nozzle assembly for moving the nozzle assembly between the first position and the second position. 12. The cleaning apparatus of claim 11, wherein the manual control interface comprises a knob on an axis, wherein rotation of the knob causes rotation of the nozzle assembly about an axis of rotation. 13. The cleaning apparatus of claim 11, further comprising a visual indicator coupled to the nozzle assembly, wherein movement of the visual indicator is correlated to movement of the nozzle assembly, and wherein the visual indicator is visible from outside of the housing. 14. The cleaning apparatus of claim 13, wherein the visual indicator comprises a plurality of markings indicative of portions of the working face of the instrument head, wherein positioning the visual indicator at one of the plurality of markings comprises moving the nozzle assembly such that the nozzle directs the stream of fluid towards a portion of the working face of the instrument head that correlates with the selected one of the plurality of markings. 15. The cleaning apparatus of claim 1, wherein the tubular instrument is an endoscope. 16. The cleaning apparatus of claim 14, wherein the working face of the instrument head lies in a plane parallel an axial direction of the instrument head. 17. The cleaning apparatus of claim 1, wherein the receiving space is configured to direct the working face of the instrument head in a downward direction, wherein the nozzle assembly is positioned to direct the stream of fluid towards the working face of the instrument head from below the instrument head. 18. The cleaning apparatus of claim 1, further comprising a pressurized fluid source coupled to the nozzle assembly to supply the fluid to the nozzle. 19. The cleaning apparatus of claim 18, wherein the pressurized fluid source is coupled to the nozzle assembly through tubing, the tubing comprising a region of flexible tubing within the housing, the region of flexible tubing having a length sufficient to permit the nozzle assembly to move between the first position and the second position. 20. A method, comprising: receiving an instrument head of a tubular instrument within a receiving space of a housing of a cleaning apparatus, the instrument head having a working face; securing a tube of the tubular instrument with a clamp coupled to the housing, wherein securing the tube of the tubular instrument comprises establishing a watertight seal around the tube of the tubular instrument; supplying pressurized fluid to a nozzle assembly within the housing; directing a stream of fluid through a nozzle of the nozzle assembly in response to supplying the pressurized fluid; and moving the nozzle assembly between a first position and a second position, wherein the nozzle directs the stream of fluid towards a first portion of the working face of the instrument head when the nozzle assembly is in the first position, and wherein the nozzle directs the stream of fluid towards a second portion of the working face of the instrument head when the nozzle assembly is in the second position. 21. The method of claim 20, wherein the housing comprises a receiving block containing the receiving space and a collection chamber fluidly coupled to the receiving space, and wherein the method further comprises collecting effluent, in the collection chamber, from the stream of fluid contacting the working face of the instrument head. 22. The method of claim 21, wherein the nozzle assembly is positioned within the collection chamber. 23. The method of claim 20, wherein the receiving space is sized to accept an end of an endoscope. 24. The method of claim 20, further comprising securing the instrument head within the receiving space. 25. The method of claim 20, wherein moving the nozzle assembly between the first position and the second position comprises rotating the nozzle assembly rotatable about an axis of rotation. 26. The method of claim 25, wherein the axis of rotation is orthogonal to an axial direction of the instrument head. 27. The method of claim 25, wherein moving the nozzle assembly between the first position and the second position further comprises translating the nozzle assembly in a direction parallel the axis of rotation. 28. The method of claim 27, wherein translating the nozzle assembly comprises translating the nozzle assembly between a first endpoint and a second endpoint, wherein the distance between the first endpoint and the second endpoint is less than a diameter of the instrument head. 29. The method of claim 28, wherein the distance between the first endpoint and the second endpoint is correlated to a width of an elevator portion of the instrument head. 30. The method of claim 20, further comprising manipulating a manual control interface coupled to the nozzle assembly, wherein moving the nozzle assembly between the first position and the second position occurs in response to manipulating the manual control interface. 31. The method of claim 30, wherein the manual control interface comprises a knob on an axis, wherein rotation of the knob causes rotation of the nozzle assembly about an axis of rotation. 32. The method of claim 30, wherein moving the nozzle assembly further comprises moving a visual indicator coupled to the nozzle assembly, wherein movement of the visual indicator is correlated to movement of the nozzle assembly, and wherein the visual indicator is visible from outside of the housing. 33. The method of claim 32, wherein the visual indicator comprises a plurality of markings indicative of portions of the working face of the instrument head, and wherein moving the nozzle assembly further comprises moving the nozzle assembly to position the visual indicator at one of the plurality of markings, which correlates to moving the nozzle assembly such that the nozzle directs the stream of fluid towards a portion of the working face of the instrument head that correlates with the selected one of the plurality of markings. 34. The method of claim 33, wherein moving the nozzle assembly between the first position and the second position further comprises moving the nozzle assembly such that the visual indicator is moved to each of the plurality of markings. 35. The method of claim 20, wherein the tubular instrument is an endoscope. 36. The method of claim 35, wherein the working face of the instrument head lies in a plane parallel an axial direction of the instrument head. 37. The method of claim 20, wherein receiving the instrument head within the receiving space comprises directing the working face of the instrument head in a downward direction, and wherein directing the stream of fluid through the nozzle comprises directing the stream of fluid towards the working face of the instrument head from below the instrument head. 38. The method of claim 20, wherein supplying the pressurized fluid to the nozzle comprises supplying the pressurized fluid through tubing, the tubing comprising a region of flexible tubing within the housing, the region of flexible tubing having a length sufficient to permit the nozzle assembly to move between the first position and the second position.
